title: Her first marathon is for her dad and grandfather — lifelong South Boston residents
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Olivia Dennett, 22, from Wilmington is running her first marathon in the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Dennett is raising funds for South Boston Neighborhood House, also known as The Ollie.
Her run honors her late grandfather and father, both lifelong residents of South Boston.

title: She’s running Boston for her mom and cancer research
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Sophie Goad is participating in the 2026 Boston Marathon to support the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.
Goad runs to honor her mother, a cancer survivor, and aims to raise $15,124 for cancer research.
Funds raised benefit the Claudia Adams Barr Program in Innovative Basic Cancer Research at Dana-Farber.

title: Jaylen Brown says underwater training with a surfing legend helped him deal with anxiety
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown credits underwater training with surfing legend Laird Hamilton for helping him manage anxiety and pressure.
The training involved simulating oxygen deprivation, teaching relaxation under pressure and mental coping strategies.
Brown has achieved a career-high performance, averaging 28.8 points and contributing to the Celtics' playoff success.

title: Police search for owner, dog after man and dog are attacked in Everett
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Everett police seek the owner of a dog involved in an attack on another dog and its owner on Ferry Street.
The injured dog, named Mushroom, required 50 stitches and surgeries; both the dog and its owner are recovering.
The owner of the attacking dog fled the scene before authorities arrived; police are investigating to identify them.

title: Mass. House passes bill restricting teen social media and phones in schools
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts House approved a bill, 129-25, banning cell phones in public schools and restricting under-16 social media access.
House bill requires parental consent for social media use by 14- and 15-year-olds; children under 14 are completely restricted.
Social media companies must establish age verification systems; school districts must update cell phone policies annually.

title: How Boston.com readers feel about the Boston Marathon not making this list of best marathons
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: 77% of Boston.com readers believe the Boston Marathon should have been included in USA Today's best marathons list.
Readers emphasize the Boston Marathon's prestige, historical significance, and challenging qualification standards as reasons for its importance.
Critics argue the event has become too commercialized and elitist, detracting from its reputation as an elite marathon.

title: Increase in bear encounters prompts new requirements for White Mountains visitors
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Pemigewasset Wilderness visitors must use bear canisters for food storage starting May 1 to prevent bear encounters.
New Hampshire's Forest Service offers free bear canister loans at several ranger stations to comply with new regulations.
Bear incidents in Pemigewasset Wilderness surged due to improper food storage, prompting the introduction of canister requirements.

title: Southbridge woman tried to hit teen with car while driving with daughter, police say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Samantha Angers, 39, charged with assault and reckless endangerment after allegedly trying to hit a teen with her car.
Incident followed a juvenile altercation, with Angers reportedly driving aggressively with her daughter in the vehicle.
Angers' bail set at $15,000; arraignment in Dudley District Court scheduled for April 30.

title: Industrial explosion sends multiple people to the hospital in East Providence
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: An explosion at Aspen Aerogels in East Providence sent 11 employees to the hospital; all have been released.
The incident, declared a mass casualty, led to a regional emergency response and nearby hospitals being alerted.
The cause of the explosion is under investigation; traffic was diverted from Dexter Road during emergency operations.

title: Red Sox rookie Tyler Samaniego achieved historic feat in MLB debut, dedicated performance to late father
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Tyler Samaniego made his MLB debut for the Red Sox, striking out three batters in his first inning.
Samaniego dedicated his performance to his late father, saying it felt like his father was with him.
The Red Sox won 5-0 against the Brewers, completing a two-out-of-three series win at Fenway Park.
